fields.definition_origin: Codified in 2024-2025 wellness app teardowns (Calm, Headspace, Sleep Cycle, Oura, Whoop) and productivity onboarding case studies (Sunsama, Cron/Notion Calendar). The pattern: the onboarding flow branches based on hour-of-launch (and optionally day-of-week, locale-adjusted). First documented as a discrete pattern in Mobbin teardowns Q2 2024 and the Adapty 2026 contextual-onboarding section. Roots: Stanford Persuasive Tech Lab's context-aware persuasion research (BJ Fogg 2003) + circadian behavioral matching (Roenneberg 2012 chronotype literature).
fields.core_mechanism: Pairs the user's CURRENT temporal context with the app's intended use-case framing, exploiting two mechanisms: (a) availability heuristic (Tversky-Kahneman 1973) — the experience of opening at 8pm makes 'evening calm practice' feel more salient and applicable than a generic 'choose when to practice' prompt; (b) situated cognition (Lave 1988) — onboarding copy that names the current moment ('Tonight feels heavy — let's lighten it') reduces abstraction and increases perceived relevance. Mechanism stack: (1) detect current local hour at app launch; (2) branch FSM to time-aware variant of opening screens; (3) modify microcopy + iconography + featured demo to match the time of day; (4) preload time-of-day-appropriate content for the AI Plan Reveal (item 12).
fields.neuroscience_correlate: Suprachiasmatic nucleus (SCN) circadian rhythm engages prefrontal evaluation of evening vs morning content as differentially relevant. Diurnal cortisol curve — peak at 30 min post-wake, nadir around bedtime — correlates with availability of mental energy for productivity vs wellness framings. [uncertain on direct fMRI evidence in onboarding-specific contexts]
fields.empirical_evidence: Calm 2024 onboarding (designerup.co teardown): evening-launched users routed to a sleep-focused flow exhibit 22% higher D7 retention vs control. Headspace 2024: morning-launched users see a focus/anxiety flow; evening users see sleep flow — internal data attributed to ~12% completion lift. Sleep Cycle: time-of-day variants (evening = sleep-onset content; morning = wake-quality reflection) cited in 2024 product blog as +15% conversion. Sunsama (productivity): morning-launched users see weekday-planning flow; weekend-launched see weekly-review flow — practitioner reports of 8-12% relative completion lift. Oura: chronotype-aware onboarding tied to Day-30 retention improvement. [Most evidence is industry case-study; few peer-reviewed RCTs of time-of-day onboarding specifically.]
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: Onboarding completion rate by hour-of-launch cohort; D1/D7 retention by chronotype variant; AI plan reveal acceptance rate by temporal context match; paywall conversion delta vs time-agnostic control.
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: Calm 2024: +22% D7 retention from evening-routed cohort vs control (single-app case study). Headspace 2024: ~12% completion lift attributed to time-of-day branching. Sleep Cycle: +15% conversion (practitioner-reported). Sunsama: 8-12% relative completion lift on weekday vs weekend variants.
fields.engineering_tactics: (1) Capture user's local hour at app launch: `const localHour = new Date().getHours();` (locale-respecting). (2) Categorize into chronotype bands: morning (5-11), midday (11-15), afternoon (15-18), evening (18-22), late-night (22-5). (3) Branch FSM at the welcome state: `welcome.morning` / `welcome.evening` / etc., with shared downstream states (personalize, reveal, paywall) but variant copy + demo content. (4) Persist `launch_hour_band` in onboarding context for cross-step stitching at the AI Reveal — '"You launched in the evening — here's a sleep-focused plan"'. (5) Day-of-week modifier: weekday vs weekend variants for productivity categories. (6) Locale-aware: launch hour interpreted in user's timezone, not server's; respect daylight saving. (7) DO NOT fork the FSM excessively — branch only welcome + first 1-2 demo screens; downstream personalization is locale+identity-driven, not time-driven.
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Calm 2024: evening launches surface 'Tonight feels heavy — let's lighten it' opener with sleep-focused soundscape demo; morning launches surface 'Start your day grounded' opener with breath-focused demo. (2) Headspace 2024: morning users see 'Anxiety / Focus / Sleep' chip selection with default 'Focus'; evening users see same chips with default 'Sleep'. (3) Sleep Cycle: evening users routed to sleep-onset content + sleep-tracking permission ask; morning users routed to wake-quality reflection. (4) Sunsama: weekday-morning launch → 'Plan today' flow; weekend launch → 'Reflect on your week' flow. (5) Oura: chronotype questionnaire (morning person / night owl) routes to early-bird vs late-riser sleep coaching. (6) Cron/Notion Calendar: weekday-morning launch routes to 'today's first meeting' setup; weekend launch routes to weekly review.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-primary because mobile is where local-hour detection is most reliable (device hardware time, no proxy/VPN distortion) and because mobile launches are more temporally diverse than web (web tends to be desk-bound 9-5 patterns). Web equivalent: time-of-day variants on dashboard onboarding; less effective because web sessions tend to cluster.
fields.platform_design_language_alignment: Liquid Glass (iOS 26+) — translucent material adapts beautifully to evening dim-mode contexts; bolder left-aligned headers work for both 'Good morning' and 'Tonight' framings. M3 Expressive (Android 16+) — expressive shape system + 4× faster element discovery work across temporal contexts. Both platforms support Live Activities / Live Updates that surface time-of-day-relevant content post-onboarding.
fields.category_fit: STRONGEST: wellness/habit (Calm, Headspace, Sleep Cycle, Oura — directly maps to use case), fitness (morning workout vs evening recovery), productivity (weekday planning vs weekend review for Sunsama, Cron, Notion). ACCEPTABLE: journaling (morning intention vs evening reflection — Reflectly), meditation (broader category fit), edtech (study-time recommendations). WEAK: AI-wrapper (time of day rarely matters), social network (always-on use), dating (time of day signal weak), gaming (24h sessions). EXCLUDE: fintech (KYC + compliance dominates; time-of-day is noise), utilities (no use-case mapping).
fields.tooling_options: React Native: built-in `Date` API for hour detection; `react-native-localize` for timezone handling. XState v5: `welcome` state with parallel substates per chronotype band. PostHog: cohort by `launch_hour_band` property. Adapty: time-aware paywall variants via Placement targeting rules. Plotline / Superwall: support contextual onboarding variants via remote config. Native iOS: `Calendar.current.component(.hour, from: Date())`. Native Android: `LocalTime.now().hour`.
fields.ai_dependency: optional: Time-of-day branching works with hardcoded variant copy (Calm, Headspace use this). LLM can enrich the AI Plan Reveal by passing `launch_hour_band` as a context input — generates time-aligned plans. Latency / cost: minimal additional impact since branching is at variant level, not per-call.
fields.claude_design_application: Prompt fragment 1: 'Build a time-of-day-aware welcome screen for [category] app. Detect local hour at launch; branch to one of five chronotype bands: morning (5-11) / midday (11-15) / afternoon (15-18) / evening (18-22) / late-night (22-5). Each band has variant headline + body + demo content. For wellness category: morning = grounding/breath; evening = sleep/calm; afternoon = focus/anxiety. Implement using XState v5 parallel substates within the welcome state. Pass `launch_hour_band` into onboarding context for downstream cross-step stitching.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Forbid: hardcoded "Good morning!" greeting that fires at 11pm. Forbid: forking the entire FSM by time of day (only welcome + first 1-2 demo screens differ). Forbid: timezone bugs from server-side time interpretation; always use device local time.'
fields.stitch_application: Prompt fragment: 'Generate 5 variant welcome screens for the same wellness onboarding flow, one per chronotype band: (1) MORNING — soft amber palette, sunrise-toned hero photography, headline "Start grounded", demo = 60-sec breath practice. (2) MIDDAY — bright warm palette, energetic photography, headline "Reset your focus", demo = 30-sec focus reset. (3) AFTERNOON — golden palette, headline "Carry the day lighter", demo = anxiety-release breathing. (4) EVENING — deep blue/violet palette, twilight photography, headline "Tonight feels heavy — let''s lighten it", demo = wind-down soundscape. (5) LATE-NIGHT — near-black palette, headline "You''re still up. Let''s help you land", demo = sleep-onset breath. Same downstream personalize/reveal/paywall flow for all variants. Forbid Inter / indigo-500 / generic Lottie sun-and-moon icons.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI codegen typically (1) hardcodes 'Good morning!' that fires regardless of time, (2) forks the entire FSM by time-of-day creating 5× engineering overhead, (3) uses server time instead of device local time (timezone bugs), (4) defaults to 2-band split (day vs night) which loses the late-night and afternoon nuances, (5) over-uses generic sun/moon icon Lottie (slop). Counter-prompt: 'Implement 5-band chronotype branching only at welcome + first 1-2 demo screens; downstream FSM is shared. Use device local hour, not server. Forbid generic sun/moon Lottie icons; use archetype-aligned imagery from emotional_spec.md.'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) Greeting that fires regardless of time ('Good morning!' at 11pm). (2) Excessive FSM forking — 5× engineering cost without proportional UX gain. (3) Server-time interpretation — produces timezone bugs for travelers. (4) Time-of-day branching for categories where it doesn't apply (fintech, utility). (5) Over-aggressive — branching paywall pricing by time of day reads as manipulative under DFA. (6) Ignoring locale — 8pm in Manila is not 8pm in NYC; same band logic applies but content may differ culturally.
fields.regulatory_risk: Low: time-of-day routing is a standard personalization pattern under GDPR/CCPA. Risk elevates if time-of-day data is combined with biometric/behavioral profiling on minors (COPPA expanded definition 2025). DFA Q4 2026 may flag aggressive temporal-pricing manipulation but standard onboarding-content variants are safe. Locale-respecting timezone handling required for GDPR compliance (no leaky cross-jurisdiction inferences).
fields.platform_constraints_2026: iOS App Store Guideline 5.1.1(v) — collect time-of-day data only as needed for declared core function. Android target API 35 (deadline 2025-08-31). No specific Apple/Google rule against time-of-day onboarding branching; standard pattern.
fields.accessibility_implications: VoiceOver/TalkBack must announce time-of-day-relevant content correctly per locale. Screen-reader users may have non-standard schedules — provide manual override ('I'm a night owl' / 'Use morning content') affordance. Color palette variants per time band must maintain WCAG 2.2 § 1.4.3 4.5:1 contrast in all chronotype variants (evening dim palettes can fail contrast if not audited). prefers-reduced-motion respected per band's animations.
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) Travelers crossing timezones — app launched at 9am local but user is jet-lagged: provide chronotype override ('Set your usual rhythm'). (2) Shift workers — night-shift workers' 'morning' is at 6pm; default branching misroutes. Mitigation: chronotype questionnaire override. (3) Daylight saving — handled by locale-respecting `Date` API but verify in test. (4) Cold launch latency — local hour detection is synchronous and instant; no perf concern. (5) Users in liminal hours (5am, 11pm) — band boundaries can feel arbitrary; use soft-edge content rather than hard cutoffs.
fields.cross_cultural_validity: Strong universal applicability with locale tweaks. MENA/Muslim contexts: prayer times (Fajr/Dhuhr/Asr/Maghrib/Isha) override standard chronotype bands — 'morning' may begin at 4am in summer. JP: workdays often extend to 8pm — 'evening' band starts later. ES/MX/IT: late-evening dinner culture means 'late-night' begins after 11pm. Mau Baron Prayer Lock localizes time-of-day to prayer hours rather than chronotype bands in MENA markets.
fields.prescribed_action: Branch the welcome state into 5 chronotype bands (morning 5-11 / midday 11-15 / afternoon 15-18 / evening 18-22 / late-night 22-5) with variant copy + demo content + color palette. Pass `launch_hour_band` into onboarding context for downstream cross-step stitching. Provide a manual chronotype override for shift workers and travelers. Use device local time, never server time. Apply only to wellness, fitness, productivity, journaling categories — exclude fintech, utility, AI-wrapper, social.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: Time-of-day branching reduces the questionnaire load — instead of asking 'When do you want to use this?' as a question, the app pre-defaults to the current time-of-day. The user can confirm or override in 1 tap, saving questionnaire steps.
fields.aha_relevance: Time-of-day-aware welcome creates an early micro-Aha ('this app already knows my context') before the engineered peak. Compounds with cross-step stitching at the AI Plan Reveal — 'You launched in the evening, so here's a sleep-focused plan' feels orders of magnitude more personal than a generic plan.
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: STRONG: Caregiver (gentle adaptive context awareness), Sage (informed wisdom — knows what time is right for what), Magician (transformation tied to temporal context). ACCEPTABLE: Lover (relatedness via context-awareness), Innocent. WEAK: Outlaw (rule-breaking archetype reads chronotype as conformist), Hero (mastery doesn't care about time of day). Pairs with .agent/research/emotional-design/results/Calm_Technology.json (calm tech embraces context).
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against generic 'Good morning!' greeting that fires regardless of time. Pairs against Wall-of-Text antipattern — chronotype branching shrinks copy load by pre-contextualizing. Aligns with Cross-Step Personalization Stitching (item 14), AI Plan Reveal (item 12), Story-Onboarding Doctrine (item 18).
